metric.visitDaysNote: Multi-day totals are visit-days (daily rotating hash)

overview.noAlerts: No recent alerts on this project.

home.title: Portfolio
home.noProjects: No projects yet — create one through the admin API.

goals.title: Goals
goals.name: Name
goals.empty: No goals yet — declare a page or an event to track conversions.
goals.error: Could not save the goal (admin role and a valid target are required).

funnel.title: Funnel
funnel.sessionNote: Anonymous funnels are limited to a single session/day — the daily rotating hash makes longer anonymous sequences impossible by design.

retention.title: Retention
retention.empty: No identified cohorts in this period — retention needs identify() and an activation event.
retention.identifiedNote: Identified users only: anonymous cross-day retention is impossible by design (the daily salt is destroyed) — that is what keeps Oriflux consent-banner-free.

vitals.title: Web Vitals
vitals.note: p75 over the selected period — thresholds per Google guidance (LCP 2.5s / CLS 0.1 / INP 200ms / TTFB 800ms).

infra.title: Infrastructure (Zeus)
infra.note: Live snapshot of {{service}} from Zeus — usage vs resources at a glance.

ask.placeholder: Ask Oriflux… e.g. which countries grew this month?
ask.disabled: AI is not configured on this instance.
ask.cannotCompile: Could not turn that question into a query — try rephrasing.
ask.title: Ask Oriflux
ask.hint: ⌘K / Ctrl+K to open — Esc to close. Every answer cites the executed query.

projectSettings.connectorsHint: Wire Stripe / Lemon Squeezy webhooks to correlate revenue with traffic (MRR on the home).
projectSettings.secretOnce: The secret is encrypted at rest and never shown again. Configure the webhook URL on the provider side.
projectSettings.connectorsDisabled: Connectors are disabled on this instance (no Fernet key).
projectSettings.zeusHint: Map this project to its Zeus service to correlate infra (CPU/RAM) with the API views.
projectSettings.zeusUnavailable: Service mapped, but Zeus is unreachable (or this instance has no Zeus).

subtitle.home: All your products at a glance — live visitors, trends and recent alerts.
subtitle.overview: The product summary: web, API and real time over the selected period.
subtitle.web: Your site's audience — visitors, pages, sources and geography, cookie-free.
subtitle.api: Your API traffic — volume, errors and latency by endpoint, consumer and country.
subtitle.live: The last 30 minutes of activity, refreshed every 10 seconds.
subtitle.product: Funnels and retention — step-by-step conversions and identified-user cohorts.
subtitle.goals: Your conversion goals — key pages or events tracked over the period.
subtitle.alerts: Thresholds on your metrics, notified on Slack or by email as soon as they are crossed.
subtitle.annotations: Releases, campaigns and incidents — the context behind chart variations.
subtitle.projectSettings: Sources, revenue connectors and Zeus integration for this project.
subtitle.orgSettings: Projects, keys, members, shares, plan and billing for the organization.

emptyState.webTitle: No web events in this period
emptyState.webBody: Paste this snippet into your site's <head>: data shows up within seconds, without cookies or a consent banner.
emptyState.apiTitle: No API traffic in this period
emptyState.apiBody: Install the Python SDK and add the middleware to your FastAPI app — under one millisecond of overhead per request.
emptyState.keyOnce: The key will never be shown again — copy the snippet now.
emptyState.issueFailed: Could not issue a key (admin role required).

pageviews.short: Total pages viewed. Every page load counts, even if the same visitor views several.

visitors.short: Distinct people, not pages. Cookieless: a visitor is recognised via a fingerprint that changes every day.
visitors.note: Deduplicated exactly within a single day; across days the same visitor may be recounted (the fingerprint rotates at midnight). That's the cost of being cookieless.

sessions.short: Number of visits. A visit groups a visitor's activity and ends after 30 minutes of inactivity.

bounce_rate.short: Share of visits that saw only one page before leaving. A high rate can signal a poorly matched landing page.

session_duration.short: Average visit length, from the first to the last recorded event.
session_duration.note: Single-page visits have a duration of 0 (no second event to measure against) — they drag the average down.

custom_events.short: Number of custom events fired (clicks, sign-ups, purchases… whatever you instrument yourself).

custom_event_visitors.short: Distinct people who fired at least one custom event.

revenue.short: Sum of the amounts attached to events (e.g. purchase value). Requires sending a value with the event.

web_vital_lcp_p75.short: LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) at the 75th percentile: how long the biggest element takes to render. Under 2.5s is good.
web_vital_lcp_p75.note: P75: 75% of your visitors get a value at least this good. It's the threshold Google uses to judge experience.

web_vital_cls_p75.short: CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) at the 75th percentile: visual stability while the page loads. Under 0.1 is good.

web_vital_inp_p75.short: INP (Interaction to Next Paint) at the 75th percentile: responsiveness to interactions. Under 200ms is good.

web_vital_ttfb_p75.short: TTFB (Time To First Byte) at the 75th percentile: time to the first byte from the server. Measures server/network latency.

api_requests.short: Number of requests your API received over the period.

api_error_rate_4xx.short: Share of requests failing with a 4xx (client-side: bad, unauthorised or not-found request).

api_error_rate_5xx.short: Share of requests failing with a 5xx (server-side: your backend crashed or didn't respond). Watch this closely.

api_latency_p50.short: Median response time (p50): half of requests are faster, half slower.

api_latency_p95.short: 95th-percentile response time: 95% of requests are faster. Reflects the slow-request experience, not the extremes.

api_latency_p99.short: 99th-percentile response time: only 1% of requests are slower. Reveals the long tail (locks, timeouts).

country.short: Visitor's country, inferred from the IP at collection time. The IP is resolved then never stored.

region.short: Visitor's region/state, inferred from the IP at collection (never stored).

city.short: Visitor's city, inferred from the IP at collection (never stored).

asn.short: Visitor's network/operator (Autonomous System Number): identifies the ISP or host, handy for spotting data-centre traffic.

page.short: The page path (URL without the domain), e.g. /pricing. Query parameters are normalised.

referrer.short: The site the visitor came from (the page that sent them to you).

utm_source.short: The URL's utm_source parameter: the campaign source (e.g. newsletter, google).

utm_medium.short: The utm_medium parameter: the campaign channel (e.g. email, cpc, social).

utm_campaign.short: The utm_campaign parameter: the marketing campaign name.

device.short: Device type: desktop, mobile or tablet, inferred from the browser.

os.short: Visitor's operating system (Windows, macOS, iOS, Android…).

traffic_class.short: The nature of the traffic: human, crawler, or AI agent. Separates real visits from automated traffic.
traffic_class.note: Classified from the user-agent (a crawler/AI-agent list maintained by Oriflux). A bot disguised as a browser can pass as human.

event_name.short: The custom event's name (e.g. signup, purchase).

class_reason.short: Why Oriflux classified this traffic as it did (e.g. the user-agent pattern that flagged a bot). Makes classification explainable.

endpoint.short: The API route called, with parameters normalised (e.g. /users/{id}), to group similar requests together.

method.short: The request's HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE…).

consumer.short: Who calls your API, identified by the API key used. Lets you see usage per client/application.

glossaryUi.title: Glossary
glossaryUi.subtitle: What every number means, in plain language.

overview.purpose: A product at a glance: its key web and API numbers for the period, its trend and its latest signals.
overview.howToRead: Tiles compare the period to the previous one (▲/▼). The chart shows the trajectory; signals list recent alerts and annotations.
overview.nextAction: Spot a change, then dig in via the Web, API or Live screens.

web.purpose: Your site's audience: visitors, pageviews, sessions, sources, pages and geography.
web.howToRead: Tiles summarise the period; tables rank by page, source, country… Click a row to filter the whole screen.
web.nextAction: If everything is zero, paste the oriflux.js snippet on your site (see Product settings).

api.purpose: Your API's health: request volume, error rate and latency, by endpoint and consumer.
api.howToRead: Watch 5xx errors and p95 latency. Tables break it down by endpoint, method and status.
api.nextAction: No data? Install the ASGI SDK in your backend (see docs).

live.purpose: What's happening right now: active visitors and events from the last few minutes.
live.howToRead: The view refreshes every 10s; the counter shows visitors active now.
live.nextAction: Open your site in a tab to watch yourself appear here.

goals.purpose: Your conversions: goals (a page reached or an event) and their completion rate.
goals.howToRead: Each goal shows its completions and rate over the period.
goals.nextAction: Create a goal from a key page or a custom event.

alerts.purpose: Your monitoring: rules that notify you when a metric crosses a threshold.
alerts.howToRead: Each rule shows its metric, condition and recent triggers.
alerts.nextAction: Create a rule (e.g. 5xx errors > 1%) with Slack or email notification.

annotations.purpose: The markers on your timeline: deploys, campaigns, incidents.
annotations.howToRead: Annotations appear on the charts so you can tie a change to a known event.
annotations.nextAction: Add an annotation on a deploy or campaign date.

home.purpose: Your portfolio: every product at a glance, with its signals.
home.howToRead: Each card summarises a product; insights surface notable changes.
home.nextAction: Open a product to see its detailed Overview.

product.purpose: A product's home: access to its web, API and live screens and its settings.
product.howToRead: Use the navigation to move between domains; the time context is preserved.
product.nextAction: Pick Web, API or Live depending on what you want to analyse.

orgSettings.purpose: Organisation settings: members and roles, keys, billing and quotas.
orgSettings.howToRead: Each section handles one aspect: invite members, manage keys, track usage.
orgSettings.nextAction: Invite a teammate, or check your usage against the plan quota.

projectSettings.purpose: Product settings: sources, ingest keys, snippet, connectors and public view.
projectSettings.howToRead: Each source (web/api) has its key; the ready-to-paste snippet is here.
projectSettings.nextAction: Copy the oriflux.js snippet or the SDK key to instrument this product.

docsUi.title: Documentation
docsUi.subtitle: The Oriflux guides, right in the app.
